items.title: Cryptographic Failures
items.description: Cryptographic Failures (OWASP A02:2021) covers weak algorithms, poor TLS config, insecure password storage, and secrets leaking in code — all of which can expose sensitive data.

items.title: Hashcat
items.description: One-liner: GPU-accelerated password hash cracking tool supporting dozens of hash types including JWT, MD5, bcrypt, and NTLM.

items.title: JWT — JSON Web Token
items.description: JWT is a three-part base64-encoded token used for authentication, but misconfigurations like weak secrets or the alg:none attack can allow attackers to forge tokens.

items.title: TLS & Certificates
items.description: TLS creates an encrypted channel between client and server using a certificate trust chain — misconfigured TLS exposes users to interception and weakens the security of every feature built on top of it.
